formulas.name: 1. Definition of One-Party Dominance
formulas.formula: A system where one party holds significant power and influence over the political landscape
formulas.notes: Used to describe the political scenario in India from 1947 to 1967

formulas.name: 2. Characteristics of One-Party Dominance
formulas.formula: Concentration of power, limited opposition, and a dominant party with broad appeal
formulas.notes: Identifies key features of a one-party dominant system

formulas.name: 3. Nehru's Contribution to One-Party Dominance
formulas.formula: Visionary leadership, charismatic personality, and policy initiatives that shaped the Congress party's dominance
formulas.notes: Highlights Nehru's role in establishing the Congress party's dominance

formulas.name: 4. Factors Contributing to One-Party Dominance
formulas.formula: Historical legacy, social and economic development, and institutional factors
formulas.notes: Analyzes the factors that contributed to the Congress party's dominance

formulas.name: 5. Impact of One-Party Dominance on Opposition Parties
formulas.formula: Limited political space, restricted mobilization, and lack of access to resources
formulas.notes: Examines the effects of one-party dominance on opposition parties

formulas.name: 6. Role of Institutional Factors in One-Party Dominance
formulas.formula: Electoral system, party organization, and state machinery
formulas.notes: Investigates the institutional factors that contributed to the Congress party's dominance

formulas.name: 7. Challenges to One-Party Dominance
formulas.formula: Regionalism, caste and communalism, and economic disparities
formulas.notes: Identifies the challenges that emerged to the Congress party's dominance

formulas.name: 8. Decline of One-Party Dominance
formulas.formula: Weakening of the Congress party, rise of regional parties, and increased political competition
formulas.notes: Describes the factors that led to the decline of one-party dominance

formulas.name: 9. Impact of One-Party Dominance on Indian Democracy
formulas.formula: Stability and governance, but also limited political participation and representation
formulas.notes: Evaluates the impact of one-party dominance on Indian democracy

formulas.name: 10. Legacy of One-Party Dominance
formulas.formula: Shaping of India's political landscape, influence on party politics, and impact on governance
formulas.notes: Assesses the lasting impact of one-party dominance on Indian politics
